description:
Red Lion Crimson 3.0 is open. An automotive transmission gear manufacturer is commissioning a new integral-quench carburizing furnace line. The furnace PLC communicates via Modbus TCP. You are the controls engineer. Your task is to configure the Crimson HMI panel to connect to the PLC and display all critical process parameters, while keeping software-computed values as local tags.

1. Navigate to the Communications section and add a new Modbus TCP/IP master device. Configure the device with Name: 'Furnace_PLC', IP Address: 192.168.10.20, Port: 502, and Unit ID: 1.
2. Navigate to the Data Tags section and create all 8 process monitoring tags listed in the reference documents.
3. For the 6 PLC-mapped tags: set the data type to Float, configure the engineering range (min/max), set the unit label, enter the description, set alarm thresholds, and map each to the 'Furnace_PLC' device at the specified holding register address.
4. For the 2 LOCAL tags (HRC_601 and CT_601): set data type to Float, configure the ranges, labels, and alarms, but leave their Source as local/internal (DO NOT map them to any Modbus device).

Both reference documents (the tag register and the AMS 2750F Modbus specification) are located on the Desktop in the CrimsonTasks folder. Once all configuration is complete, save the project exactly as 'heat_treat_modbus.c3' in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\.

Login credentials: Docker / GymAnything123!
